evidence_summary: Type I interferons (the family IFN-omega belongs to) are well-documented inducers of IFN-gamma in NK/NKT/T cells, and IFN-omega specifically enhances cytolytic CD8+ T cell IFN-gamma responses. The relay through pDC is plausible since pDCs are the primary type I IFN amplifier.
citations: Type 1 Interferon Induction of Natural Killer Cell Gamma Interferon Production during LCMV Infection — https://journals.asm.org/doi/10.1128/mbio.00169-11
Human type-I interferon omega promotes cytolytic CD8+ T cell responses (bioRxiv 2025) — https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2025.02.13.638030v1
IFN-alpha treatment stimulates IFN-gamma in type I NKT cells — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5333814/
search_queries_used: IFN-omega induces IFN-gamma plasmacytoid dendritic cells
type I interferon induces IFN-gamma NK cells T cells
IFN-omega pleiotropic immune cells review

evidence_summary: OSM is known to expand Treg and modulate Th17/Th2 balance, but no published evidence directly links OSM to IL-9 induction in Treg or other immune cells.
search_queries_used: OSM oncostatin M induces IL-9 Treg
OSM oncostatin M IL-9 production immune cells

evidence_summary: APRIL is known to act on B cells and induce IL-10/PD-L1 in regulatory B cells, but no direct evidence found for APRIL induction of IL-20 in monocytes.
search_queries_used: APRIL TNFSF13 induces IL-20 monocytes
APRIL BAFF induces IL-20 cytokine production

evidence_summary: IL-35 and IL-27 are both heterodimeric IL-12-family cytokines sharing the Ebi3 subunit; both function as negative regulators with overlapping receptor usage (gp130/IL-12Rb2/WSX-1), but A→B direction (IL-35 inducing IL-27) is not explicitly documented.
citations: Interleukin-35 on B cell and T cell induction and regulation — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5547520/
IL-12 family cytokines: immunological playmakers (Nature Immunology) — https://www.nature.com/articles/ni.2366
search_queries_used: IL-35 induces IL-27 CD8 T cells
IL-35 IL-27 relationship IL-12 family co-regulation

evidence_summary: No literature found linking IL-9 to decorin expression in immune cells. Decorin is an ECM proteoglycan with TGF-beta scavenger function; no IL-9-related regulatory axis documented.
search_queries_used: IL-9 induces decorin proteoglycan CD8 T cells
IL-9 decorin expression immune cells

evidence_summary: VEGF modulates T cell IL-10/IFN-gamma balance and influences immunosuppression, but no published evidence found for VEGF induction of IFN-lambda1 (IL-29) in CD8 or any immune cell type.
search_queries_used: VEGF induces IFN-lambda1 IL-29 expression T cells
VEGF interferon lambda induces inhibits T cells

evidence_summary: CD30L is expressed on activated monocytes (LPS-inducible) and IL-27 has effects on monocyte inflammatory programs, but no direct evidence found that IL-27 induces CD30L/TNFSF8 expression on monocytes.
search_queries_used: IL-27 induces CD30L TNFSF8 monocytes

evidence_summary: IL-27 is documented to suppress Th2 responses (including IL-13 production), so the documented direction is reverse (IL-27 → suppresses IL-13). IL-27 is known to induce IL-10 in MAIT cells, but IL-13 inducing IL-27 in MAIT was not found.
citations: Interleukin-27 in T Cell Immunity — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4346869/
Human MAIT cell response profiles biased toward IL-17 or IL-10 — https://www.pnas.org/doi/10.1073/pnas.2414230122
search_queries_used: IL-13 induces IL-27 MAIT cells
IL-13 IL-27 expression macrophages T cells

evidence_summary: IL-6 is a well-established inducer of VEGF expression via trans-signaling and STAT3 activation, both directly and synergistically with IL-1B and TNF.
citations: Interleukin 6 induces the expression of vascular endothelial growth factor — https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/8557680/
IL-6 in Inflammation, Immunity, and Disease — https://cshperspectives.cshlp.org/content/6/10/a016295.full
IL-6 induces VEGF-C via Src-FAK-STAT3 — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4934912/
search_queries_used: IL-6 induces VEGF production T cells

evidence_summary: The documented direction is reverse: IL-36-alpha induces IL-6 (in fibroblasts and synovial cells, via MyD88/NF-kB). IL-6 inducing IL-36-alpha is not the documented cascade.
citations: IL-36a and IL-36g Induce Proinflammatory Mediators from Human Colonic Subepithelial Myofibroblasts — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4585048/
IL-36a Exerts Pro-Inflammatory Effects in the Lungs of Mice — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3447790/
search_queries_used: IL-6 induces IL-36-alpha inflammation

evidence_summary: The documented direction is reverse: OX40L is a potent inducer of IL-9 (TH9 induction via TRAF6/NF-kB). IL-9 inducing OX40L is not the documented cascade direction.
citations: OX40 signaling favors the induction of TH9 cells and airway inflammation (Nature Immunology) — https://www.nature.com/articles/ni.2390
IL-33-primed human mast cells drive IL-9 production by CD4+ T cells in an OX40L-dependent manner — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC11479898/
search_queries_used: IL-9 induces OX40L CD4 T cells

evidence_summary: GM-CSF differentiates and primes myeloid cells; neutrophils are known to secrete APRIL in response to stimuli (TGF-beta, glycosaminoglycans), but a direct GM-CSF → APRIL induction (especially via NKT cells) is not directly documented. Indirect link via myeloid activation.
citations: Neutrophil TLR4 and PKR are targets of glycosaminoglycans and effectors of APRIL secretion — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6002394/
search_queries_used: GM-CSF induces APRIL TNFSF13 production NKT
GM-CSF induces APRIL myeloid neutrophils

evidence_summary: IL-27 has been shown to augment TNF-alpha-induced IL-6 production in epithelial cells; the IL-6/IL-27 axis is well-documented bidirectional within the gp130 cytokine family, with both directions reported.
citations: Early IL-6 signalling promotes IL-27 dependent maturation of regulatory T cells — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5633202/
Regulation of inflammatory immune responses by IL-27 — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3332455/

IL-27 IL-6 macrophages mutual regulation

evidence_summary: Both VEGF and IL-19 participate in vascular inflammation but no direct evidence found for VEGF inducing IL-19 in NK cells or other immune cells.
search_queries_used: VEGF induces IL-19 NK cells

evidence_summary: Decorin has documented immunomodulatory effects on monocytes via MCP-1 induction and macrophage polarization, but no published evidence found linking decorin to IL-16 induction.

evidence_summary: VEGF affects T-cell function (IL-10/IFN-gamma balance, suppression via VEGFR2), and NKT cells are known producers of IL-21, but no published direct link of VEGF inducing IL-21 in NKT cells was found.
search_queries_used: VEGF induces IL-21 NKT cells

evidence_summary: IL-9 is a known ILC2 product induced by IL-33/IL-25 alarmins, but no published evidence found that IL-19 induces IL-9 in ILCs.

evidence_summary: IL-16 stimulation of CD14+ CD4+ monocytes/maturing macrophages induces IL-1b, IL-6, IL-15, and TNF-alpha production, with mRNA detected within 4 hours and protein by 24 hours. This is a direct, well-documented A→B effect.
citations: Interleukin-16 stimulates the expression and production of pro-inflammatory cytokines by human monocytes — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC2326980/
search_queries_used: IL-16 induces IL-6 monocyte production

evidence_summary: APRIL modulates T cell proliferation and Th2/Th17 cytokine output in mouse models (in APRIL-/- mice, Th2 cytokines increase), but no published direct evidence of APRIL inducing IL-12 in CD4 T cells.
citations: APRIL (TNFSF13) regulates collagen induced arthritis, IL-17 production and TH2 response — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC2755626/
search_queries_used: APRIL TNFSF13 induces IL-12 T cell CD4

evidence_summary: IL-15 induces monocyte/macrophage activation and chemokine production (IL-8, MCP-1), and APRIL is produced by monocytes/macrophages. Both share myeloid expression but no direct mechanistic evidence found for IL-15 induction of APRIL in CD14 monocytes.
citations: Interleukin-15 (IL-15) Induces IL-8 and Monocyte Chemotactic Protein 1 Production in Human Monocytes — https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0006497120579937
